rationale: Both confirm the deploy-override pipeline-resolution fix and flag the trust_remote_code default flip, but Y catches more of the ground-truth concerns: it independently spots the else-branch that silently skips even the always-on UNSUPPORTED_ROUTES guard (echoing the maintainers' 'audit other endpoints for crashes' ask more concretely than X's speculative Qwen2.5-Omni parity claim), and touches on cache-invalidation scope and the error-response fallback pattern, both adjacent to real inline comments (mutability of cached hf_config, reuse of create_error_response). X's findings are slightly more precisely line-cited and its 'sweep' validations are useful noise-reduction, but its top 'major' finding pushes to revert trust_remote_code to True, contradicting the author's explicit stated rationale for the change. Precision and actionability are close between the two, but Y's broader, better-targeted recall makes it the stronger review.
